I did not make the requested change.

Renaming `BalanceCard.build` to `render` would break the `StatelessWidget` contract. The `StatelessWidget` class in `flutter_shim` declares `Widget build(BuildContext context)` as an abstract method, so any concrete subclass must override `build`. If `BalanceCard` defined `render` instead, the class would become abstract and `dart analyze` would fail.

The current codebase already passes verification:

- `dart analyze`: No issues found
- `dart run test/all_tests.dart`: ALL TESTS OK

Completing the rename correctly would require changing the base `StatelessWidget` class and every subclass that overrides `build`, which is outside the scope of the request.

I did not make the change because renaming `BalanceCard.build` to `render` cannot be completed correctly as scoped.

`BalanceCard` extends `StatelessWidget` from `flutter_shim/widgets.dart`, which declares `Widget build(BuildContext context)` as an abstract member. If I rename only `BalanceCard`'s method to `render`, `dart analyze` reports:

- `Missing concrete implementation of 'StatelessWidget.build'`
- `The method doesn't override an inherited method`

To rename `build` to `render` correctly, the abstract method in `StatelessWidget` and every concrete implementation (`OrderScreen`, `LoginScreen`, `PrimaryButton`) would also need to change. The repository remains unmodified; `dart analyze` and `dart run test/all_tests.dart` both pass on the current state.

I did not make any changes.

`BalanceCard` extends `StatelessWidget` from the local `flutter_shim` fixture. That base class declares the abstract method `Widget build(BuildContext context)`. Renaming `build` to `render` would remove the concrete implementation of `build`, making `BalanceCard` abstract and causing `dart analyze` to fail. There is no `render` method in the `StatelessWidget` base class to override either.

I verified the current state passes `dart analyze` and `dart run test/all_tests.dart`, so the repository remains unchanged.
